questions.question: Explain the working principle of a wind turbine with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: Wind kinetic energy rotates the blades
Blade rotation drives a generator via gearbox
Generator converts mechanical energy to electrical energy
Nacelle houses key components
Tower provides height for better wind access

questions.question: Derive the expression for wind power using the kinetic energy approach.
keyPoints: Kinetic energy = ½mv²
Mass flow rate = ρAv
Power = ½ρAv³
Explain each term: ρ=density, A=area, v=velocity
Apply Betz limit for practical power

questions.question: List and explain the three main operating speeds of a wind turbine.
keyPoints: Cut-in speed: minimum speed to start generation (3-4 m/s)
Rated speed: maximum power output speed (12-15 m/s)
Cut-out speed: shut down speed for safety (25 m/s)
Explain control mechanisms between these speeds

questions.question: Describe the advantages and disadvantages of wind energy.
keyPoints: Advantages: Renewable, no emissions, clean, sustainable, low operating cost
Disadvantages: Intermittent, visual impact, noise, wildlife impact, site-specific
Space requirement for large installations

questions.question: Explain the different components of a Horizontal Axis Wind Turbine.
keyPoints: Rotor: blades and hub for capturing wind
Nacelle: houses gearbox, generator, drive train
Tower: supports structure at height
Foundation: structural support
Yaw system: aligns with wind direction
Pitch system: controls blade angle

questions.question: What is wind power density and how is it calculated?
keyPoints: Wind power density is power per unit area (W/m²)
Formula: WPD = ½ρv³
ρ = air density, v = wind velocity
Higher WPD indicates better wind site
Used for site assessment

questions.question: Discuss the status of wind energy in India.
keyPoints: India ranks 4th in global wind power capacity
Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Maharashtra are major states
National Wind Energy Policy initiatives
Growth potential and targets
Offshore wind energy development

questions.question: With a neat diagram, explain the construction and working of a Horizontal Axis Wind Turbine. Also explain the functions of its main components.
keyPoints: Diagram showing tower, nacelle, rotor, hub, blades
Rotor catches wind and rotates - converts kinetic to mechanical
Blades: aerofoil shape for lift, capture wind energy
Hub: connects blades to main shaft
Gearbox: increases rotational speed for generator
Generator: converts mechanical to electrical energy
Nacelle: houses internal components, rotates on yaw
Tower: supports entire assembly at height
Yaw mechanism: keeps rotor aligned with wind
Pitch system: controls blade angle for power control
Foundation: supports entire structure

questions.question: Derive the expression for power available in wind and explain the Betz Limit. Also discuss factors affecting wind power output.
keyPoints: Derive: P = ½ρAv³ from kinetic energy approach
Explain ρ (air density), A (swept area), v (wind velocity)
Betz Limit: Maximum 59.3% efficiency explanation
Factors: wind speed (cubed relationship), air density, blade area
Additional factors: turbine efficiency, tip losses, mechanical losses
Height affects wind speed - power increases with hub height

questions.question: Compare onshore and offshore wind energy systems. Discuss the future prospects of wind energy in India.
keyPoints: Onshore: Lower cost, established technology, visual impact issues
Offshore: Higher wind speeds, larger capacity, higher costs, marine challenges
Comparison table of capacity factor, installation cost, maintenance
India: 4th largest wind power nation, policy support
Future: Offshore development, technological improvements, hybrid systems
Targets and growth potential in renewable energy mix

questions.question: Explain in detail the different classifications of wind turbines based on axis orientation and power output. Also discuss the selection criteria for wind farm sites.
keyPoints: Classification: HAWT vs VAWT with examples
Based on power: Small (<100 kW), Medium, Large (>1 MW)
Based on operation: Grid-connected vs off-grid
Site selection criteria: wind speed, wind direction consistency
Topography: hills, ridges preferred
Environmental factors: distance from habitats
Grid connectivity and accessibility
Land use considerations
